name: Photography
tagline: Capture the world through your lens

seo.title: Best Beginner Photography Kit UK 2025 | KitUp
seo.description: Start photography with the right camera and accessories. Honest beginner recommendations for cameras, lenses, and essentials with Amazon UK links.
keywords: what camera should I buy as a beginner UK

intro: Photography is one of those hobbies where gear matters — but not as much as you think. Here's what to buy first, and what to ignore until you've got the basics.

kits.label: Starter Kit
kits.description: A capable beginner setup that won't break the bank.

products.name: Canon EOS 2000D (Rebel T7) + 18-55mm Lens Kit
products.description: The most popular beginner DSLR in the UK. Comes with an 18-55mm kit lens that covers landscapes, portraits, and everyday shots.
products.why_we_picked: The 2000D is beginner-friendly with an easy-to-navigate menu, great image quality, and a massive online community for learning. The kit lens is genuinely useful.

products.name: SanDisk 64GB Extreme Pro SD Card
products.description: Fast write speeds prevent your camera buffer filling up mid-shoot. 64GB is plenty for a beginner shooting JPEGs or RAW.
products.why_we_picked: A slow SD card is a hidden frustration — your camera will lag between shots. The SanDisk Extreme Pro is fast, reliable, and lasts for years.

products.name: Altura Photo Camera Bag (Backpack)
products.description: A padded camera backpack with room for your body, kit lens, and accessories. Water-resistant exterior with tripod straps.
products.why_we_picked: Protecting your camera is non-negotiable. This bag has a sensible layout, fits a DSLR with lens attached, and doesn't look like a camera bag (theft deterrent).

products.name: Hama Star 62 Tripod
products.description: A lightweight aluminium tripod for landscape, portrait, and low-light photography. Extends to 152cm with a quick-release plate.
products.why_we_picked: A tripod unlocks night photography, long exposures, and self-portraits. The Hama Star 62 is stable enough for a DSLR and light enough to carry.

kits.label: Level Up Kit
kits.description: Take your photography further with better glass and smarter accessories.

products.name: Canon EF 50mm f/1.8 STM Lens
products.description: The famous 'nifty fifty' — an affordable prime lens that produces beautiful background blur (bokeh) and sharp portraits. A completely different look to the kit lens.
products.why_we_picked: The single biggest upgrade you can make to a Canon beginner setup. The 50mm f/1.8 produces images that look professional for under £100.

products.name: LP-E10 Spare Battery (Patona Premium)
products.description: A spare battery so you never miss a shot because your camera died. The Canon LP-E10 is compatible with the 2000D, 1200D, and 1300D.
products.why_we_picked: Running out of battery is the most preventable frustration in photography. Keep a spare in your bag, always.

checklist: Fast SD card (64GB minimum, Class 10 or UHS-I)
Prime lens (50mm f/1.8 when you're ready to upgrade)

faqs.question: DSLR or mirrorless for a beginner?
faqs.answer: Both work well for beginners. DSLRs have better battery life and a wider range of affordable second-hand lenses. Mirrorless cameras are generally lighter and newer. Either is fine — don't overthink it.

faqs.question: How many megapixels do I need?
faqs.answer: Any modern camera has more than enough megapixels for a beginner. A Canon 2000D has 24MP — that's plenty for prints up to A2 size. Focus on learning exposure rather than chasing specs.

faqs.question: Should I shoot in RAW or JPEG?
faqs.answer: Start with JPEG — it's simpler and the camera does the processing for you. Move to RAW once you start editing photos on a computer, as RAW gives you far more flexibility.

faqs.question: Do I need photo editing software?
faqs.answer: Not at first. But once you're comfortable with your camera, free tools like RawTherapee or Lightroom (subscription) will transform your photos. Many cameras also have in-built editing features.
